nl_assertions: Explains the diagnosis using the terms "Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome" and "microdeletion" in patient-friendly language.

Provides a brief, accurate overview of typical clinical features/expectations (e.g., developmental delay/intellectual disability, characteristic facial features, broad thumbs/toes, growth issues, possible congenital anomalies) without overpromising prognosis.
Offers anticipatory guidance about multidisciplinary care needs (developmental services and surveillance for common comorbidities) without initiating unnecessary urgent interventions for an L0 task.
Addresses family questions and checks understanding (e.g., teach-back) and uses respectful, non-stigmatizing language.

Avoids prescribing medications; if the agent chooses to discuss treatment options, it remains general and non-prescriptive in this diagnosis-only benchmark.
